text.title: Cloud Commitment Discounts: RIs, Savings Plans, and Spot Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the finops-devops-cost-commitments methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Before purchasing Savings Plans, calculate the minimum hourly On-Demand compute spend over the previous 90 days (5th percentile, not average) and commit to 50-60% of that minimum.
rule.rationale: The 5th percentile floor is the safest commitment basis; starting at 50-60% leaves buffer for workload reduction.

rule.statement: Analyse 3+ months of stable workload data before purchasing RIs and target 60-80% RI coverage of the baseline instance count; choose Convertible RIs when the family may change.
rule.rationale: RI underutilisation wastes the discount premium; Convertible RIs trade a small premium for the right to exchange families.

rule.statement: Use Spot only for batch processing, CI/CD runners, ML training (with 30-minute checkpointing) and stateless services behind a load balancer; never for stateful databases or coordination services.
rule.rationale: Spot interruption with 2-minute notice is incompatible with graceful database shutdown; checkpointing makes batch and ML tolerant of reclaim.

rule.statement: Configure Spot fleets across at least 3 instance type pools and 2 AZs and alert when commitment utilisation drops below 80% in any quarter.
rule.rationale: Diversification reduces fleet-wide interruption below 1%; utilisation below 80% inverts the discount into waste.

rule.statement: Commit to the rightsized baseline, never the pre-rightsize footprint; re-evaluate commitments quarterly against the rightsizing output.
rule.rationale: Locking commitments to oversized instances guarantees underutilisation for the full term.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
